options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: puzzle_count
options.description: Number of puzzles to randomly generate.

options.name: starting_puzzles
options.description:
Number of puzzles to randomly add to the starting inventory. These will be
removed from the item pool.

options.name: completion_percentage
options.description:
Percent of puzzles which must be completed to finish the world, rounding up.

If you set this to a low value, it is HIGHLY RECOMMENDED to disable release on world completion.

options.name: min_difficulty
options.description:
Minimum difficulty to select generated presets from. Note that not all genres have presets at "Hard" difficulty,
so they will never appear if the minimum difficulty is set to Hard (unless you specify custom presets for them).

options.name: max_difficulty
options.description:
Maximum difficulty to select generated presets from. Takes priority over the Minimum Difficulty option if it is lower.

Puzzles on "Ridiculous" difficulty are either very big or not actually randomized. Use this difficulty at your own risk.

options.name: genre_weights
options.description:
Relative chance for each genre to be selected as a random preset. Set a genre's
weight to 0 or remove it from this list to disable it.

options.name: genre_min_difficulty
options.description: Minimum difficulty for each genre. Use 0 for easy, 1 for normal, 2 for hard, and 3 for ridiculous.

options.name: genre_max_difficulty
options.description: Maximum difficulty for each genre. Use 0 for easy, 1 for normal, 2 for hard, and 3 for ridiculous.

options.name: preset_overrides
options.description:
List of presets to use for a given genre. Replaces the default presets for
that puzzle if specified.

For example, to make Net puzzles always use wrapping grids, add:
net: [5x5w, 7x7w, 9x9w, 11x11w]

options.name: fixed_puzzles
options.description:
List of additional puzzles to include. These puzzles will be placed at the start of the list.
The remaining list (up to puzzle_count) will be filled according to genre_weights.

You can specify by genre (net), parameter string (net:2x2), seed (net:2x2#12345), or ID (net:2x2:c494).

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: exclude_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an important item.

options.name: priority_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an unimportant item.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.

options.name: start_inventory_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
